methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
ParityQC is a Series A-stage quantum computing company founded by Wolfgang Lechner and Magdalena Hauser. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 comparable companies—3 publicly traded (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion. However, the peer-median valuation method is not applicable here due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables: zero same-stage Series A peers, only one adjacent-stage peer, and zero similar-valuation-tier matches were identified. While 7 same-sector companies exist, none satisfy the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for robust peer-median analysis. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias given the heterogeneity in quantum modalities, development phase, and commercial readiness across the universe.

Given data constraints, traditional triangulation methods cannot be reliably applied. The analysis cannot produce a defensible valuation range without additional financial data on the identified peers (revenue multiples, burn rates, or disclosed funding rounds at comparable stages) or disclosure of ParityQC's own operational metrics (revenue, customer traction, burn rate). Any range offered would rest on insufficient empirical grounding. The key uncertainty is the lack of adequate same-stage quantum computing comparables with disclosed valuations. Future valuation work should prioritize obtaining Series A funding rounds and revenue data from the 8 private-sector peers to establish meaningful benchmarks.
